text:
Market today: ETF inflows hit a record pace in H1 2026, meaning retail and institutional money is still chasing tech/semis aggressively. Oil's biggest quarterly drop in 6 years is deflationary - good for tech multiples. Mood is risk-on but stretched; your names are in premium territory.

Your holdings: All green, no SELL/TRIM flags. Two things to watch: SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged semi ETF - on margin that's effectively massive exposure; a semi reversal hits you 3x plus your margin rate. SPAL is brand-new and very volatile - it's a leveraged ETF, moves fast, decays in chop, and on margin a bad day compounds quickly. Everything else looks fine today.

Watch: (1) SMH as the cleanest read on semi momentum - if it fades, SOXL pain arrives fast. (2) Whether NVDA holds the $198 area; it's grade-C setup but a bellwether for your whole book.

Bottom line: You're fully long, on margin, at all-time-high territory - protect what's working; no need to add heat today.
plays: SOXL: your stop is $200 - honor it hard. On margin + 3x leverage, a semi pullback from premium levels is portfolio-level damage fast.
SPAL: brand-new leveraged ETF, very volatile. Default stop is $21.38 - keep it tight or trim to reduce margin exposure on an untested name.
No new buys today - scanner setups exist but no fresh catalyst specific to your holdings justifies adding margin risk into premium territory right now.
